title: Notifying the Post Office and Redirecting Mail
summary: Redirecting mail is a small step that prevents a lot of downstream confusion and missed paperwork.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Notifying the Post Office and Redirecting Mail | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Redirecting mail is a small step that prevents a lot of downstream confusion and missed paperwork.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Mail forwarding can be set up online or in person.
Someone should monitor incoming mail for anything time-sensitive.
This is worth doing even before all other notifications are complete.

paragraphs: Setting up mail forwarding to a family member's address ensures nothing important — a bill, a tax document, a benefits notice — gets missed simply because it went to an empty home.

Reviewing forwarded mail for a few months afterward tends to reveal accounts or subscriptions that weren't otherwise on anyone's radar, making it a genuinely useful ongoing step, not just a one-time task.

nextSteps: Set up mail forwarding as early as reasonably possible.
Assign one person to review incoming mail regularly.
Watch specifically for anything time-sensitive or unfamiliar.
disclaimer: General guidance — mail forwarding services and timelines are set by the postal service directly.
